vars.finding:
[0] [bug] pkg/storage/unified/search/bleve.go:104-141
  Description: Race condition introduced by reducing lock scope in BuildIndex. The previous code held cacheMu for the entire function, serializing all index builds and preventing duplicate work for the same key. The new code only locks during cache assignment, allowing multiple goroutines to concurrently build indexes for the same NamespacedResource key. For file-based indexes, this causes both goroutines to attempt creating files at the same directory path, leading to potential file corruption and resource leaks.
  Existing code: b.cacheMu.Lock() // moved to only cache write
  Suggested fix: Restore lock at function start or implement per-key locking

[0] [bug] pkg/api/webassets/webassets.go:48-52
  Description: Missing second check in double-checked locking pattern. After acquiring the write lock at line 48-49, the code must re-verify entryPointAssetsCache != nil before proceeding to load assets. Without this check, when multiple goroutines race on a cold cache, all of them will sequentially acquire the lock and redundantly compute the same result, wasting resources.
  Existing code: entryPointAssetsCacheMu.Lock() defer Unlock()
  Suggested fix: Add if entryPointAssetsCache != nil { return } after lock

[0] [bug] pkg/expr/reader.go:195-200
  Description: Logic error in enableSqlExpressions: The function always returns false regardless of the feature flag state. The variable enabled is assigned the negated value of the feature flag check (!h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(...)), and then both conditional branches return false. This makes SQL expressions impossible to enable even when the FlagSqlExpressions feature toggle is globally enabled.
  Existing code: enabled := !h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(featuremgmt.FlagSqlExpressions)
  Suggested fix: return h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(featuremgmt.FlagSqlExpressions)

[0] [bug] public/app/plugins/datasource/loki/shardQuerySplitting.ts:52-55
  Description: Template variables and ad-hoc filters are applied twice when shard splitting is used. runShardSplitQuery applies applyTemplateVariables with request.filters, then calls runSplitQuery which applies them again. The subRequest spread copies request.filters to the subRequest, causing duplicate ad-hoc filter injection.
  Existing code: const interpolated = queries.map(q => datasource.applyTemplateVariables(q, request.scopedVars, request.filters))
  Suggested fix: Remove applyTemplateVariables from runShardSplitQuery since runSplitQuery handles it

[0] [security] pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go:116-121
  Description: Cache consistency issue: permDenialCache is checked before permCache, and both have independent 30-second TTLs. When permissions are granted after a denial was cached, users remain denied for up to 30 seconds even though permCache would return fresh permissions.
  Existing code: if denied, ok := s.permDenialCache.Get(key); ok { return false }
  Suggested fix: Check permCache first, invalidate denial entries on permission changes, or store denials inline in permCache

metadata.case: Denial cache not invalidated after grant

vars.finding:
[0] [bug] app/controllers/admin/embeddable_hosts_controller.rb:18
  Description: Nil reference vulnerability in destroy action. EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first returns nil if the record doesn't exist, and the code immediately calls host.destroy without checking. This causes a NoMethodError and reveals information via error messages.
  Existing code: host = EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first; host.destroy
  Suggested fix: return render_json_error(404) unless host

[0] [security] apps/web/pages/api/webhook/app-credential.ts:29
  Description: Timing attack vulnerability in webhook secret comparison. The direct string comparison using !== short-circuits on first mismatch, allowing attackers to measure response times and guess the secret character by character.
  Existing code: if (req.headers[headerName] !== process.env.CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
  Suggested fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual with constant-time comparison

[0] [bug] packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleSMSReminders.ts:43
  Description: Logic error in deleteMany query: The OR condition's second branch lacks method: WorkflowMethods.SMS filter. This causes unintended deletion of EMAIL and WHATSAPP reminders that have retryCount > 1, violating the stated purpose of only deleting SMS reminders.
  Existing code: OR: [{ scheduledDate: { lte: ... } }, { retryCount: { gt: 1 } }]
  Suggested fix: OR: [{ scheduledDate: { lte: ... } }, { retryCount: { gt: 1 }, method: WorkflowMethods.SMS }]

metadata.case: OR condition deletes wrong reminder types

vars.finding:
[0] [bug] src/sentry/api/paginator.py:877-882
  Description: The OptimizedCursorPaginator allows negative offset slicing on Django QuerySets when enable_advanced_features is True. Django QuerySets do not support negative indexing like Python lists - attempting to use negative indices will cause an AssertionError or database error.
  Existing code: queryset[cursor.offset:stop]
  Suggested fix: Validate offset is non-negative before slicing

[0] [bug] src/sentry/replays/endpoints/project_replay_summarize_breadcrumbs.py:140
  Description: The get_request_data function mutates the input error_events list in-place with .sort(). This causes side effects that could affect callers who don't expect their list to be modified.
  Existing code: error_events.sort(key=lambda x: x['timestamp'])
  Suggested fix: Use sorted() instead to return a new sorted list

[0] [performance] app/controllers/admin/groups_controller.rb:75
  Description: N+1 query in add_members. Loop calls User.find_by_username for each username individually. With 1000 usernames, this executes 1000 database queries instead of a single batch query.
  Existing code: usernames.each { |u| User.find_by_username(u) }
  Suggested fix: User.where(username: usernames)

[0] [security] app/controllers/groups_controller.rb:22
  Description: Unbounded limit parameter allows DoS attacks. The limit parameter is converted to integer but has no maximum value validation, allowing attackers to request massive record sets that overload the database.
  Existing code: limit = params[:limit].to_i
  Suggested fix: limit = [params[:limit].to_i, 100].min

[0] [security] pkg/expr/sql/parser.go:26
  Description: SQL injection vulnerability: using naive single-quote escaping and fmt.Sprintf to build SQL commands is unsafe. The strings.Replace(rawSQL, "'", "''", -1) only handles single quotes but doesn't prevent other injection vectors. User-controlled rawSQL is injected directly into the command string.
  Existing code: fmt.Sprintf("SELECT json_serialize_sql('%s')", strings.Replace(rawSQL, "'", "''", -1))
  Suggested fix: Use parameterized queries or proper SQL parsing

[0] [performance] pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go:116
  Description: Unbounded cache growth in permDenialCache due to resource-specific keys. The cache key includes name and parentFolder which have high cardinality (unique per resource). For a system with 1000 users accessing 1000 different resources, this creates ~1M unique cache entries that persist for 30 seconds.
  Existing code: s.permDenialCache.Set(userPermDenialCacheKey(namespace, userUID, action, name, parent), true)
  Suggested fix: Remove per-resource denial caching or add size limits

[0] [security] apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/setup.ts:61
  Description: Backup codes use only 40 bits of entropy (5 bytes). Should use at least 80 bits for resistance to brute force attacks.
  Existing code: crypto.randomBytes(5).toString('hex')
  Suggested fix: crypto.randomBytes(10).toString('hex')

[0] [security] packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/refreshOAuthTokens.ts:14
  Description: CALCOM_CREDENTIAL_SYNC_ENDPOINT environment variable used in fetch without URL validation. Could allow SSRF if environment variable is compromised.
  Existing code: fetch(process.env.CALCOM_CREDENTIAL_SYNC_ENDPOINT, { method: 'POST', body: params })
  Suggested fix: Validate URL protocol and domain before fetching

[0] [security] apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/disable.ts:52
  Description: Missing rate limiting on backup code verification endpoint allows brute force attacks against the 10 backup codes.
  Existing code: // no rate limit check before processing backup code
  Suggested fix: Add checkRateLimitAndThrowError(req) before verification
